annotator_1.text: Foundation models based on prior-data fitted networks (PFNs) have shown strong empirical performance in causal inference by framing the task as an in-context learning problem.
annotator_1.source_quote: Foundation models based on prior-data fitted networks (PFNs) have shown strong empirical performance in causal inference by framing the task as an in-context learning problem.

annotator_1.text: Existing PFNs, when interpreted as Bayesian ATE estimators, can exhibit prior-induced confounding bias.
annotator_1.source_quote: We show that existing PFNs, when interpreted as Bayesian ATE estimators, can exhibit prior-induced confounding bias

annotator_1.text: The prior is not asymptotically overwritten by data, which, in turn, prevents frequentist consistency.
annotator_1.source_quote: the prior is not asymptotically overwritten by data, which, in turn, prevents frequentist consistency.

annotator_1.text: The OSPC helps to restore frequentist consistency.
annotator_1.source_quote: We show that the OSPC helps to restore frequentist consistency

annotator_1.text: OSPC can yield a semi-parametric Bernstein-von Mises theorem for calibrated PFNs.
annotator_1.source_quote: We show that the OSPC helps to restore frequentist consistency and can yield a semi-parametric Bernstein-von Mises theorem for calibrated PFNs

annotator_1.text: Both the calibrated PFN-based estimators and the classical semi-parametric efficient estimators converge in distribution with growing data size.
annotator_1.source_quote: both the calibrated PFN-based estimators and the classical semi-parametric efficient estimators converge in distribution with growing data size

annotator_1.text: PFNs calibrated with our martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that asymptotically matches frequentist uncertainty.
annotator_1.source_quote: PFNs calibrated with our martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that (i) asymptotically matches frequentist uncertainty

annotator_1.text: PFNs calibrated with our martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that is well calibrated in finite samples in comparison to other Bayesian ATE estimators.
annotator_1.source_quote: PFNs calibrated with our martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that (i) asymptotically matches frequentist uncertainty and (ii) is well calibrated in finite samples in comparison to other Bayesian ATE estimators.

annotator_2.text: Foundation models based on prior-data fitted networks (PFNs) have shown strong empirical performance in causal inference by framing the task as an in-context learning problem.
annotator_2.source_quote: Foundation models based on prior-data fitted networks (PFNs) have shown strong empirical performance in causal inference by framing the task as an in-context learning problem.

annotator_2.text: It is unclear whether PFN-based causal estimators provide uncertainty quantification that is consistent with classical frequentist estimators.
annotator_2.source_quote: However, it is unclear whether PFN-based causal estimators provide uncertainty quantification that is consistent with classical frequentist estimators.

annotator_2.text: Existing PFNs, when interpreted as Bayesian ATE estimators, can exhibit prior-induced confounding bias.
annotator_2.source_quote: We show that existing PFNs, when interpreted as Bayesian ATE estimators, can exhibit prior-induced confounding bias

annotator_2.text: The prior in existing PFNs is not asymptotically overwritten by data.
annotator_2.source_quote: the prior is not asymptotically overwritten by data

annotator_2.text: The prior not being asymptotically overwritten by data prevents frequentist consistency in existing PFNs.
annotator_2.source_quote: the prior is not asymptotically overwritten by data, which, in turn, prevents frequentist consistency

annotator_2.text: A calibration procedure based on a one-step posterior correction (OSPC) can remedy prior-induced confounding bias in PFNs.
annotator_2.source_quote: As a remedy, we suggest employing a calibration procedure based on a one-step posterior correction (OSPC).

annotator_2.text: The OSPC helps to restore frequentist consistency in PFNs.
annotator_2.source_quote: We show that the OSPC helps to restore frequentist consistency

annotator_2.text: OSPC can yield a semi-parametric Bernstein-von Mises theorem for calibrated PFNs.
annotator_2.source_quote: can yield a semi-parametric Bernstein-von Mises theorem for calibrated PFNs

annotator_2.text: Both calibrated PFN-based estimators and classical semi-parametric efficient estimators converge in distribution with growing data size.
annotator_2.source_quote: both the calibrated PFN-based estimators and the classical semi-parametric efficient estimators converge in distribution with growing data size

annotator_2.text: OSPC can be implemented through tailoring martingale posteriors on top of the PFNs.
annotator_2.source_quote: Finally, we implement OSPC through tailoring martingale posteriors on top of the PFNs.

annotator_2.text: Martingale posteriors enable recovery of functional nuisance posteriors from PFNs.
annotator_2.source_quote: In this way, we are able to recover functional nuisance posteriors from PFNs

annotator_2.text: Functional nuisance posteriors are required by the OSPC.
annotator_2.source_quote: functional nuisance posteriors from PFNs, required by the OSPC

annotator_2.text: PFNs calibrated with martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that asymptotically matches frequentist uncertainty.
annotator_2.source_quote: PFNs calibrated with our martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that (i) asymptotically matches frequentist uncertainty

annotator_2.text: PFNs calibrated with martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that is well calibrated in finite samples compared to other Bayesian ATE estimators.
annotator_2.source_quote: PFNs calibrated with our martingale posterior OSPC produce ATE uncertainty that... (ii) is well calibrated in finite samples in comparison to other Bayesian ATE estimators

annotator_2.text: The authors conducted multiple (semi-)synthetic experiments to evaluate PFNs calibrated with martingale posterior OSPC.
annotator_2.source_quote: In multiple (semi-)synthetic experiments, PFNs calibrated with our martingale posterior OSPC
